description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched gable roof constructed from tan-colored shingles, with a prominent central ridge and stepped eaves. The exterior walls are clad in a combination of stonework at the base and tan wooden siding above, giving a sturdy yet inviting appearance. Multiple rectangular windows with white frames are symmetrically arranged along the sides, while the front facade includes a small entryway with a wooden door and a short path flanked by hedges. The landscaping includes a paved walkway, green shrubbery, and a small patch of grass, enhancing the residential feel. The rear of the bungalow is simpler, with fewer windows and a continuous stone base. The overall structure is compact and horizontally oriented, typical of suburban residential neighborhoods.
description: A tan, gable-roofed bungalow with stone and wood siding, symmetrical windows, and landscaped front entry. The design is compact and residential, with a welcoming suburban appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stepped gable roof, multiple rectangular windows, and the stone base with tan siding above.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, short walkway, hedges, and symmetrical window arrangement above the door.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, with visible windows and the same stepped roof structure.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a simpler facade with a single window and continuous stone base, lacking the entry features of the front.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
